_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Symptomatic patients with CTEPH require pulmonary thromboendarterectomy.

claim.label: Post procedure stills of ECHO with report

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure
Was the Echo/ Doppler report suggestive of pulmonary hypertension?
Was the CTPA report suggestive of Chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH)?
Was the Lung perfusion report suggestive of Chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH)?

cpd_claim.q: Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes missing or lacking detailed history, signs, symptoms, or indication for procedure
Echo/Doppler report missing or not suggestive of pulmonary hypertension
CTPA report missing or not suggestive of chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH)
Lung perfusion report missing or not suggestive of chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH)
Post procedure stills of ECHO with report not submitted
Detailed Discharge Summary not submitted or missing date of follow-up

clinical_key_pointers: Chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH) is a complication of venous thromboembolic disease.
Pulmonary thromboendarterectomy (PTE) remains the preferred and potentially curative option for patients with chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH).
The disease is characterized by intraluminal thrombus organization, fibrotic scar tissue-like stenosis, and the ensuing vascular remodeling of unaffected pulmonary vessels.
CTEPH symptoms are nonspecific and usually related to the development of pulmonary hypertension.
Patients may be asymptomatic for several years before presenting with progressive exertional dyspnea, chronic nonproductive cough, atypical chest pain, tachycardia, syncope and cor pulmonale.
